name: Battle of Ticino
description: A fast campaign cavalry battle on the Po Valley plain. Hannibal's mounted force must read Roman movement, choose between a direct charge, northern Numidian flank, southern pursuit cut-off, or bridgehead rush, then prevent the Roman reconnaissance force from escaping across the Ticino.
design_pillars: Keep the center readable for cavalry combat, but surround it with meaningful lanes, cutoffs, groves, irrigation channels, and bridge choices.
Every road has a destination: Roman retreat, Carthaginian flank, baggage post, farmstead, bridgehead, scout ford, or ambush pocket.
The player wins by movement, not only by killing: trapping Roman cavalry before the bridge is the core skill test.
The Roman army should feel like a reconnaissance force that tries to disengage intelligently instead of fighting to the last man in the meadow.
Props communicate gameplay: carts mark retreat lanes, tents mark camps, weapon racks mark strongpoints, ruins mark villages, dead trees mark groves and line-of-sight breaks.

weather.description: Clear autumn light over the Po Valley. Dust trails from cavalry movement should be easy to read.

rivers.description: Ticino River forming the eastern boundary and Roman escape route.
rivers.role: hard escape boundary and final objective line

rivers.description: Northern farm tributary limiting wide cavalry movement.
rivers.role: narrows the northern flank and creates scout crossing choices

rivers.description: Shallow riparian channel near the northern flank.
rivers.role: screens the Numidian route and Roman supply lane

rivers.description: Southern irrigation channel that complicates Roman retreat and Carthaginian pursuit.
rivers.role: turns southern movement into a timing and bridge-control problem

rivers.description: Small drainage ditch beside the Roman baggage road.
rivers.role: creates a final southern chase lane near the baggage post

bridges.description: Main Ticino bridge. Capturing this cuts off the Roman retreat.
bridges.role: primary escape cutoff and final capture objective

bridges.description: Northern supply bridge used by Roman scouts.
bridges.role: alternate Roman escape for scouts and northern reserve

bridges.description: Southern fallback bridge near the Roman baggage route.
bridges.role: secondary escape route and baggage post objective

bridges.description: Small field bridge over the southern irrigation channel.
Eastern field bridge on the Roman withdrawal route.
Narrow farm crossing on the northern tributary.
A shallow willow ford usable by light cavalry only.
bridges.role: advanced scout cutoff toward northern supply bridge

bridges.description: Small bridge over the baggage drainage ditch.

route_design.intended_choice: Use heavy cavalry to smash the Roman reconnaissance line before it can retreat.
route_design.risk: Roman spear support and ridge position punish a frontal attack if Numidians do not disrupt them.
route_design.reward: Fastest route to morale break and decisive victory if micro is strong.

route_design.theme: light cavalry harassment and bridge interception
route_design.intended_choice: Send horse archers through groves and tributary crossings to hit Roman archers, scouts, and northern escape routes.
route_design.risk: The route is narrow and slow for heavy cavalry; isolated Numidians can be caught by Roman northern reserve.
route_design.reward: Can silence Roman scouts, block the northern supply bridge, and reveal the main retreat timer early.

route_design.intended_choice: Use the southern wing to cross irrigation channels and reach the fallback road before the Roman rout begins.
route_design.risk: Overcommitting south weakens the center and can allow Roman cavalry to defeat the heavy vanguard.
route_design.reward: Best route to decisive victory because it traps routing Romans and threatens the baggage post.

route_design.intended_choice: Ignore part of the Roman field force and rush the main bridgehead to cut off retreat immediately.
route_design.risk: Roman cavalry can collapse onto the player's rear; unsupported units may die at the bridgehead.
route_design.reward: Locks the main escape route and forces Rome into risky northern or southern fallback paths.

route_design.intended_choice: Take the central farmstead to split Roman infantry from cavalry and gain warning of retreat direction.
route_design.risk: Slower than pure cavalry maneuvers and can pull the player into a messy fight around obstacles.
route_design.reward: Provides vision, healing/firecamp landmark, and a safe pivot between north, center, and south.

roads.purpose: main clash and Roman withdrawal line
Carthaginian heavy cavalry entry into the main fight
Numidian flank route through groves and tributary crossings
advanced light cavalry cutoff toward the northern supply bridge
connects hidden ford to Roman northern escape and supply route
late chase lane and baggage escape interception
Roman scouts and reserve use this as alternate escape
Roman fallback road if main bridge is threatened
farmstead bypass between central clash and southern cutoff
short tactical loop around farmstead props and ruins
loop around the bridgehead hill with multiple attack angles
loop around baggage hamlet and southern fallback bridge
connects player camp to southern pursuit wing
connects camp to north flank without crossing the central meadow

terrain.description: Open cavalry meadow. This is the main clash space and should stay readable. Keep large enough for charges and retreats.
Roman withdrawal basin. Once Roman cavalry breaks, this becomes the chase zone.
Carthaginian observation mound above the western approach.
Low bank near the western grove that splits the north flank entrance from the central road.
Western northern levee that protects Numidian horse archers from immediate Roman contact.
Northern levee overlooking the tributary crossing.
Low embankment at the hidden willow ford; light cavalry can use it to ambush the northern supply bridge route.
Roman cavalry ridge. Strong position at first contact, but vulnerable to being outflanked.
Small farmstead mound. It breaks line of sight and gives the player a pivot point, but it should not block cavalry movement completely.
Southern spur controlling the Roman retreat track.
Low ridge along southern farmland that hides the pursuit wing until it reaches the irrigation bridge.
Small rise above the baggage ditch bridge. A good interception point during the rout phase.
Roman bridgehead camp elevation.
Mound guarding the northern supply bridge. Good archer perch, but vulnerable from the willow ford.
Low hamlet ridge around the Roman baggage post and southern fallback bridge.
Western grove hiding the Numidian opening move.
Riparian trees screening the northern flank route.
Small central copse. Useful for breaking line of sight but not large enough to stall cavalry.
Roman retreat cover near the southern road.
Willow trees around the northern Ticino supply crossing.
Sparse orchard beside the baggage ditch, used for final pursuit line-of-sight breaks.
Po Valley farmstead in the center of the battlefield.
Roman baggage hamlet beside the southern fallback bridge.
Southern farmland used by Carthaginian cavalry to swing wide.
Northern farmland and grazing ground for Numidian maneuver.

spawns.description: Roman southern reserve near retreat road

patrols.purpose: makes the center feel alive before full engagement
pressures the northern Numidian route and reveals the supply bridge
adds life to the baggage hamlet and creates a secondary fight
visually teaches the northern route without forcing the player

side_objectives.title: Seize the Central Farmstead
side_objectives.reward: Reveal Roman retreat direction for 120 seconds after their morale breaks.
side_objectives.tradeoff: Slows the opening charge and risks fighting near props instead of open ground.

side_objectives.title: Cut the Northern Supply Bridge
side_objectives.condition: Control northern_supply_bridge_zone before Roman rout phase begins
side_objectives.reward: Roman scouts and northern reserve cannot escape north; decisive victory threshold becomes easier.
side_objectives.tradeoff: Requires committing light cavalry far from the main fight.

side_objectives.title: Hold the Southern Spur
side_objectives.reward: Roman southern retreat road is slowed and baggage post becomes vulnerable.
side_objectives.tradeoff: Overcommitting south can lose the center.

side_objectives.title: Capture the Roman Baggage Post
side_objectives.tradeoff: The baggage post is not required; chasing it too early may let the Roman cavalry escape.

side_objectives.title: Preserve the Cavalry Arm
side_objectives.condition: Win with at least 50 percent of player cavalry alive
side_objectives.reward: Campaign bonus or next-mission cavalry veterancy.
side_objectives.tradeoff: Encourages encirclement and harassment instead of simple frontal trading.

phases.name: recon_screen
phases.behavior: Roman cavalry probes the meadow but stays near the ridge and keeps retreat roads open.

phases.name: engaged
phases.starts_when: player attacks Roman units or enters central_meadow_zone
phases.behavior: Roman cavalry fights in the center while reserves guard north and south retreat lanes.

phases.name: controlled_withdrawal
phases.behavior: Roman units begin falling back toward main_ticino_bridge unless cut off.

phases.name: rout
phases.starts_when: global_morale <= 30 or roman_cavalry_ridge_zone is lost and southern_spur_zone is player-controlled
phases.behavior: Roman cavalry tries to escape through main, northern, or southern bridge depending on blocked zones.

phases.name: bridgehead_last_stand
phases.starts_when: roman_ticino_bridgehead is attacked or all escape routes are blocked
phases.behavior: Bridgehead infantry forms a final defensive line while remaining cavalry tries to break through.

retreat_route_logic.condition: main_ticino_bridge blocked and northern_supply_bridge not blocked
main_ticino_bridge blocked and southern_fallback_bridge not blocked

events.message: The Roman scouts hold the ridge ahead. Strike their front, ride north through the groves, or send the southern wing to cut off their retreat.
The farmstead is ours. From here we can read Roman movement toward the bridge roads.
The northern supply bridge is blocked. Roman scouts cannot escape that way.
The southern spur is held. Roman withdrawal through the irrigation road will be costly.
Roman horns call a withdrawal. Their cavalry is falling back toward the Ticino.
The Roman line breaks. Run them down before they reach the bridges.
The main Ticino bridgehead is ours. Rome's retreat is cut.
The Roman baggage is seized. Their withdrawal turns to panic.
Too many Roman riders are reaching the bridge. This will be a victory, but not a decisive one.

rewards.fantasy: Hannibal's cavalry proves superior and the Romans fail to carry useful intelligence back across the Ticino.
The river crossing belongs to Carthage.

prop_clusters.gameplay_role: safe army staging and route choice start
primary capture objective and escape route
secondary reward and alternate escape route

maze_readability.principle: This is not a dense infantry maze. It is a cavalry movement maze: broad lanes, visible cutoffs, soft blockers, and multiple escape routes.

branches.lure: open meadow and Roman ridge fire at [166,66]
branches.leads_to: quick morale damage and direct bridgehead pressure
branches.danger: spear screen and Roman ridge defenders

branches.lure: dead trees, tributary crossing, and broken cart near [72,32]
branches.leads_to: willow ford and northern supply bridge
branches.danger: isolated horse archers can be caught by Roman reserve

branches.leads_to: vision and flexible pivot toward center or south

branches.lure: irrigation bridge and southern spur at [146,103]
branches.danger: overcommitting south weakens the main clash

branches.lure: main road and Roman camp props at [220,66]
branches.danger: Roman cavalry can attack from behind if not routed or screened

intended_difficulty_notes: The player should win decisively by controlling routes, not by simply killing every Roman unit in the center.
The central meadow must remain open enough for cavalry micro and readable dust trails.
The northern and southern routes are not side decoration; they directly affect Roman escape logic.
Roman retreat should be readable: horn, ping, dust, and movement toward one of the bridges.
Bridgehead rush is viable but dangerous if the Roman field force is still intact.

victory.description: Defeat the Roman reconnaissance cavalry, prevent the main Roman force from escaping across the Ticino, and capture the bridgehead.

primary_objectives.description: Rout the Roman cavalry in the central meadow.
Cut off the Roman retreat before too many units reach the Ticino bridges.

secondary_objectives.description: Keep at least half of the Carthaginian cavalry alive.
Use the northern Numidian route to strike the Roman flank or cut the northern bridge.
Hold the southern spur before the Roman rout begins.

outcomes.message: Decisive victory: Roman reconnaissance is shattered before it can escape.
Partial victory: the bridgehead falls, but too many Roman riders escape with intelligence.

victory.failure_hint: If the Roman cavalry escapes across the main Ticino bridge in large numbers, the mission becomes a partial victory instead of a decisive one.

conditions.message: The Roman reconnaissance force escaped across the Ticino.
